channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'Generational shift' to unify ACT public school system amid funding woes
item.description: The ACT's public education system will be significantly reformed after a review of resourcing found schools operated much like "independent small businesses".
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: The review of public school system resourcing recommended implementing a unified approach to the system.

item.title: Rare hybrid turtle hatchlings discovered on Queensland beach
item.description: Hybrid hatchlings crossed between a loggerhead and green turtle have been found on the beach at Mon Repos turtle nesting site.
item.dc:creator: Nikki Sorbello

media:group.media:description: Hybrid loggerhead and green turtles have hatched at Mon Repos on Queensland's coast.

item.title: Wiradjuri slam energy company's 'inexcusable' damage to rock shelter
item.description: Members of a First Nations community in Central West NSW say they were devastated to learn a culturally significant rock shelter had been destroyed to make way for a renewable energy project.
item.dc:creator: Lani Oataway

media:group.media:description: The rock shelter near the Cope State Forest in the Mudgee district that was destroyed by ACEREZ workers.

item.title: Murderer convicted of 1999 crime back in custody for breaching parole
item.description: Murderer Garry Thompson has been returned to custody for 12 months for breaching his parole conditions multiple times.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Garry Thompson was jailed for life by South Australia's Supreme Court for the 1999 murder of Jodi-Ann Trueman.

item.title: Fidel Castro's daughter hopes US can bring down father's regime
item.description: Fidel Castro's daughter says the US has failed to find a way to deal with Cuba for decades. But she tells the ABC that the Trump administration has a difference that could spell the end of the regime she fled.
item.dc:creator: Kamin Gock and Phoebe Hosier

media:group.media:description: Alina Fernández with her father Fidel Castro on her wedding day.

item.title: Hungry eleven-armed seastars making a meal of Tasmanian scallops
item.description: The fast spread of pest eleven-armed seastars has fishers, along with researchers, concerned.
item.dc:creator: Laurissa Smith and Bec Pridham

media:group.media:description: It is hoped that an industry can be developed to make use of the eleven-armed seastar.

item.title: SAPOL commissioner rejects outback police shortage plan
item.description: Commissioner Grant Stevens rejects recommendations to attract more officers to the understaffed Coober Pedy station over funding concerns.
item.dc:creator: Declan Durrant and George Yankovich

media:group.media:description: Coober Pedy, about 645km south of Alice Springs, and about 850km north of Adelaide, is experiencing police staffing shortages and retention issues.

item.title: Tasmania's Bonorong Wildlife Sanctuary to star in Paramount+ series
item.description: It's not all cute and fluffy animal stories at Bonorong Wildlife Sanctuary. Its director Greg Irons says a new docuseries will show the real face of animal conservation, and it's not all "happy Larry".
item.dc:creator: Ebony ten Broeke

media:group.media:description: The series will feature eight one-hour-long episodes.

item.title: Why drones fell from the sky during Sydney's Vivid light show
item.description: The company behind the drone shows at Sydney's Vivid festival said spectators were not at risk when almost 90 unmanned aircraft fell from the sky during a display on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Lewis

media:group.media:description: Almost 90 drones crashed into Sydney Harbour and nearby during a Vivid light show.

item.title: Fire union criticises department's 'flawed' $6.2m helmet rollout
item.description: Queensland's fire minister has intervened following tensions between the firefighter union and department.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie

media:group.media:description: The then-Palaszczuk government announced the rollout of the jet-style helmets in 2023 to "improve frontline communication".

item.title: Rental crisis puts domestic violence victim-survivors in 'diabolical' situation
item.description: As Brisbane's rental market hits record high prices and historic low vacancy rates, advocates are warning some domestic violence victims are having to choose between staying in an unsafe home and facing homelessness.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bahr

media:group.media:description: Many women struggle to find safe, affordable housing after leaving domestic violence.

item.title: Reception hours slashed at police stations in Melbourne's outer-north
item.description: Victoria Police says its decision to cut the hours police stations are open to the public in Melbourne's outer north will result in more police on the frontline.
item.dc:creator: Margaret Paul

media:group.media:description: Victoria Police says the reduction in reception staffing will allow for more frontline policing.

item.title: Grants allocated for soybean and rice trials in Mossman
item.description: The Queensland government will allocate $300,000 for new soybean and rice projects in Mossman as the region continues to search for a future beyond sugar cane.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Johnson and Brendan Mounter

media:group.media:description: Drew Watson has seen good results from his first commercial soybean crop, despite the prolonged wet season.

item.title: Complaint lodged with Canada's privacy commissioner over Australian court breach
item.description: Litigants in at least 146 court matters were potentially involved in a data breach that is now the subject of a formal complaint with the privacy commissioner in Canada, Senate estimates has been told.
item.dc:creator: Heidi Davoren

media:group.media:description: Chief Executive Officer and Principal Registrar of the Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia David Pringle told Senate estimates affected litigants were yet to be notified.

item.title: Women and children linked to ISIS spend first night back in Australia
item.description: A group of so-called ISIS brides who left the notorious al-Roj camp in Syria last week have spent their first night home in Australia.

media:group.media:description: Kirsty Rosse-Emile leaves Melbourne Airport after touching down yesterday afternoon.

item.title: The BHP files reveal a big problem facing the mining giant
item.description: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.
item.dc:creator: Marian Wilkinson

media:group.media:description: Hundreds of pages of internal BHP documents, exclusively leaked to Four Corners and Guardian Australia, reveal how the company sought to publicly position itself as a climate leader while internally finding reasons to put off action.

item.title: What to do when you get unexpected ancestry DNA test results
item.description: Direct-to-consumer genetic testing kits are often given as gifts or purchased "as a bit of fun", which means people often aren't prepared to discover unexpected parents, siblings and other relatives.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Scott

media:group.media:description: Online genetic testing kits are often given as gifts, which means people often aren’t prepared to uncover family secrets.

item.title: Scott's sacking gives Essendon another chance to step out of Hird shadow
item.description: Essendon finds itself at a clear demarcation point after sacking Brad Scott as the shadow of James Hird once again looms large over the troubled club.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: James Hird again looms over Essendon's coaching search after spectacularly throwing his hat into the ring on Tuesday night.

item.title: The NSW Blues made a 'tough' selection call, so will the Wallabies
item.description: Just as NSW Blues coach Laurie Daley had a difficult selection decision to make, Joe Schmidt may be thinking long and hard about one position in the Wallabies' back line for July's Nations Championship Tests.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Max Jorgensen is a contender for the Wallabies' fullback role.

item.title: State of Origin is tonight. Here is what you need to know for Game I
item.description: The fiercest rival in Australian rugby league resumes tonight, as New South Wales and Queensland meet in the opening match of the 2026 men's State of Origin series.

media:group.media:description: State of Origin begins tonight in Sydney.

item.title: Despite growing up online, students' digital literacy is declining
item.description: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.
item.dc:creator: Conor Duffy and Catherine McAloon

media:group.media:description: Approximately 10,000 students took the NAP-ICT test in May last year.

item.title: Woman forced out of home as mould grows after insurer repairs roof
item.description: Lily lodged an insurance claim with her provider GIO Suncorp after a truck ran through her street's power lines. Nearly a decade later, she has been forced out of her home.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: Lily bought her home in the early 1970s.

item.title: Iran war derailed talks to claw back billions in tax credits paid to miners
item.description: Billions in tax credits are paid to coal miners every year. Ministers discussed clawing back some of that money in the May federal budget, but the Iran war has pushed out those talks.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: Mining companies receive billions in refunds on taxes paid on diesel each year.

item.title: Quiz: Who has scored more tries in State of Origin games in Sydney?
item.description: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

media:group.media:description: SYDNEY, AUSTRALIA - JUNE 05: James Tedesco of the Blues celebrates scoring a try with teammates during game one of the 2024 Men's State of Origin Series between New South Wales Blues and Queensland Maroons at Accor Stadium on June 05, 2024 in Sydney, Australia.

item.title: Why Blak Douglas won't enter the Archibald Prize again
item.description: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

media:group.media:description: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled Home Flown.
